data.title: France has seen 41 kidnappings targeting cryptocurrency holders this year.
data.description: France has become a hotspot for wrench attacks, with at least 41 cryptocurrency-related kidnappings and burglaries reported this year, averaging one every 2.5 days. Interior Minister Jean-Didier Berger stated that he is working with Interior Minister Laurent Nuñez to prepare a series of new measures to address the problem. A wrench attack refers to forcing victims to transfer crypto assets through physical violence. Data from Certik and Jameson Lopp shows that 72 cases of physical coercion were verified globally in 2025, a 75% increase year-on-year, with cases involving physical attacks increasing by 250%. Ledger co-founder David Balland was kidnapped in France in January 2025. Security researchers point out that attackers are shifting from searching for wallets to hunting individuals, targeting them through social media and leaked data. Because crypto transactions are irreversible, attackers often convert illicit proceeds into stablecoins and transfer them across blockchains to evade detection. Experts recommend using tools such as multi-signature, withdrawal delays, and spending limits to reduce the risk of attack.

data.title: Iran: The Strait of Hormuz is part of Iranian sovereignty, and Iran will not relinquish control of it.
data.description: According to Iranian media outlet Press TV, Ebrahim Azizi, former commander of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps and chairman of the Iranian parliament's National Security and Foreign Policy Committee, stated that Iran will never relinquish its control over the Strait of Hormuz. He asserted that this vital waterway is an integral part of Iranian national sovereignty and will always remain under Iran's complete jurisdiction. When asked if Tehran would relinquish control of the strait, Azizi replied, "Absolutely not. This is our inalienable right. Iran will determine the right of passage, including permitting ships to pass through the strait." He stated that this principle is being solidified through legislation. The senior lawmaker said, "We are introducing a bill in parliament based on Article 110 of the Constitution, covering environmental, maritime security, and national security, and the armed forces will enforce this law." Tehran now considers its ability to regulate maritime traffic through this waterway crucial for restoring deterrence and maintaining long-term strategic leverage. (Jin Shi)

data.title: Hong Kong Securities Regulator Outlines Digital Asset Milestones
data.description:
Hong Kong's Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) Executive Director of Intermediaries, Yeh Chi-heng, delivered a keynote speech at the 2026 Hong Kong Web3 Carnival. According to ChainCatcher, Yeh reviewed six significant milestones achieved since the launch of the ASPIRe roadmap last year. These include allowing licensed platforms to offer staking services, conducting joint consultations on virtual asset trading and custody systems, opening perpetual contracts and margin financing frameworks, and introducing technology-enhanced market defense plans.

Yeh disclosed that the draft regulations for virtual asset trading, custody, management, and advisory systems have reached 260 pages, with the draft received last week. Over the next 12 months, the SFC's work will focus on three main clusters: using regulation to drive innovation through legislative and regulatory guidance consultations, allowing tokenized authorized funds to trade on licensed platforms, and promoting innovation through interaction by advancing automated reporting, signing international bilateral memorandums, and enhancing frameworks to combat financial crime. He emphasized that Hong Kong is progressing steadily, moving quickly due to its robust approach.

data.title: Most crypto stocks retreated, but SocialFi and AI sectors saw slight gains.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, the cryptocurrency market as a whole trended downwards due to macroeconomic factors such as the small-scale clashes between the US and Iran on Sunday. Bitcoin (BTC) fell 1.11% in the last 24 hours, dropping to around $74,000, while Ethereum (ETH) fell 2.37%, breaking below $2,300. However, SocialFi and AI sectors saw slight gains, with 24-hour increases of 1.92% and 0.95% respectively. Within the SocialFi sector, Toncoin (TON) and Chiliz (CHZ) rose 1.49% and 4.60% respectively. In the AI ​​sector, Pieverse (PIEVERSE) surged 77.43%, and Venice Token (VVV) rose 4.89%. In other sectors, the CeFi sector fell 0.86% in the last 24 hours, but Bitget Token (BGB) rose 1.35%; the Layer 1 sector fell 1.06%, with Canton Network (CC) showing relative resilience, rising 1.64%; the PayFi sector fell 1.46%, with Monero (XMR) rising 0.41% intraday; the Meme sector fell 2.17%, with MemeCore (M) falling 6.03%; the DeFi sector fell 2.24%, with Reserve Rights (RSR) bucking the trend and rising 10.52%; and the Layer 2 sector fell 2.28%, with Arbitrum (ARB) falling 0.39%. Crypto sector indices reflecting historical sector performance show that the ssiMeme, ssiDeFi, and ssiLayer2 indices fell 3.00%, 2.70%, and 2.63%, respectively.

data.title: Hong Kong Financial Services and the Treasury Deputy Secretary Chan Ho-lim: The LEAP framework will guide Hong Kong in building a digital asset ecosystem integrated into the real economy.
data.description: In his address at the 2026 Hong Kong Web3 Carnival, Mr. Chan Ho-lim, Under Secretary for Financial Services and the Treasury, stated that the LEAP framework proposed in the "Hong Kong Digital Asset Development Policy Declaration 2.0" is a blueprint for future development, aiming to build a trustworthy, sustainable, and deeply integrated digital asset ecosystem into the real economy. This framework comprises four pillars: Legal and regulatory streamlining, expanding the suite of tokenized products, advancing use cases and cross-sectoral collaboration, and people and partnership development. Mr. Chan pointed out that through the LEAP framework, Hong Kong is advancing the regulatory system for digital asset trading and custody services, with plans to submit a draft ordinance in 2026. Simultaneously, the government will promote the tokenization of assets such as precious metals and energy, explore real-time liquidity management through the Ensemble project, and collaborate with universities and industry to cultivate a new generation of blockchain and AI technology talent.

data.title: Solana's Meteora Responds to Vercel Security Incident
data.description: On April 20, Solana ecosystem's DeFi protocol Meteora announced its response to a security incident involving cloud hosting platform Vercel. According to BlockBeats, Meteora swiftly rotated environment keys and reviewed logs 12 hours prior, confirming that everything was functioning normally. The official statement emphasized ongoing monitoring to ensure the safety of Meteora and its users.

data.title: Apple Delays Launch of New Mac Models Due to Industry-Wide Memory Shortage
data.description: Apple has postponed the release of two new Mac products due to a widespread memory shortage affecting the industry. According to Odaily, the launch of the new Mac Studio, equipped with M5 Max/Ultra chips, has been delayed until October. Additionally, the first OLED touchscreen MacBook Pro, featuring the M6 chip, is now expected to be released between late 2026 and early 2027. The delay is attributed solely to supply chain constraints and is not related to the progress of product development.

data.title: OneKey Founder Suggests Negotiation Strategy for KelpDAO Hack
data.description: OneKey founder Yishi has proposed negotiating with the hacker responsible for the KelpDAO breach, suggesting a bounty of 10–15% as the best course of action. According to NS3.AI, Yishi outlined alternative measures such as seeking support from the LayerZero ecosystem fund, offering token-based compensation, or considering a sale of the project. He emphasized that WETH depositors should not incur losses.

data.title: Morgan Stanley Advises Caution Amid Rising Tensions in Middle East
data.description: Morgan Stanley strategists have issued a warning to investors following the escalation of tensions between the U.S. and Iran over the weekend. According to Odaily, the renewed standoff in the Strait of Hormuz has reignited market caution. Investors who had recently reallocated their portfolios towards Asian and emerging market stocks are advised to begin reducing their risk exposure.

data.title: Meta to Initiate Major Layoffs in May, Shifting to AI-Driven Structure
data.description: Meta is set to begin a significant round of layoffs in May, with the first phase potentially affecting 8,000 employees. According to PANews, this move is part of the company's strategy to transition towards an AI-driven organizational structure. The decision reflects Meta's focus on integrating artificial intelligence into its operations, aiming to enhance efficiency and innovation. The layoffs are expected to be implemented in stages, aligning with the company's broader restructuring plans.

data.title: The BLESS project team has sold a total of 400 million BLESS tokens, worth approximately $4.43 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Yu Jin, a few days ago, the BLESS project team (@theblessnetwork) sold 300 million BLESS tokens, worth $3.83 million. Of these, 200 million were transferred to Bitget for sale, and 100 million were sold on the BSC chain, causing the token price to drop by 71%. Approximately four hours later, the project team sold another 100 million BLESS tokens (worth $600,000), transferring them from the Sol chain to the BSC chain. To date, they have sold a total of 37.84 million BLESS tokens, obtaining 334 BNB, worth approximately $210,000.

data.title: Memory shortage delays two new Mac models from Apple
data.description: Due to an industry-wide memory shortage, Apple has delayed the release of two new Mac products. The new Mac Studio (powered by the M5 Max/Ultra chip) is postponed to October, while the first OLED touchscreen MacBook Pro (M6 chip) is delayed until late 2026 or early 2027. This delay is solely due to supply chain bottlenecks and is unrelated to product development progress. (Jinshi)

data.title: Hong Kong Advances Digital Asset Development with Strategic Initiatives
data.description:
Hong Kong's Deputy Secretary for Financial Services and the Treasury, Chen Haolian, highlighted the region's proactive approach towards Web3 and digital assets since the release of its first digital asset development policy statement in 2022. According to Foresight News, the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region Government is committed to establishing a regulatory framework that balances risk management with industry growth, aiming to position Hong Kong as a global innovation hub for digital assets.

Hong Kong is making steady progress by optimizing legal and regulatory environments, expanding tokenized asset categories, promoting application scenarios, enhancing international cooperation, and developing the talent market. These efforts are designed to create a stable development environment for the digital asset industry while fostering deep integration with the real economy. Specific policy implementations include issuing licenses for stablecoin issuers, launching tokenized gold investment products, issuing digital bonds, and participating in the Project Ensemble sandbox initiative. These measures underscore Hong Kong's determination and practical actions in the Web3 and digital asset innovation sector.

data.title: STOCKS | Hong Kong Consumer Electronics Stocks Strengthen
data.description: Hong Kong's consumer electronics sector saw significant gains today, with several key players experiencing notable increases in their stock prices. According to Jin10, Lens Technology (06613.HK) led the charge with a 6.14% rise. AAC Technologies Holdings Inc. (02018.HK) followed with a 3.63% increase, while Cowell e Holdings Inc. (01415.HK) edged up by 0.85%. These movements reflect growing investor confidence in the consumer electronics market in Hong Kong.

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks: Qunhe Technology Surges Nearly 40%
data.description: Qunhe Technology (00068.HK) has seen a significant increase in its stock price, currently rising nearly 40% to 25.96 Hong Kong dollars. According to Jin10, this surge reflects strong market interest and investor confidence in the company's performance. The stock's upward trajectory highlights its potential in the Hong Kong market.

data.title: Morgan Stanley: Risks from the Iranian situation have escalated again; recommends reducing exposure to Asian and emerging market equities.
data.description: Morgan Stanley strategists say that renewed market caution stems from escalating tensions between the US and Iran over the weekend and a renewed standoff in the Strait of Hormuz. Investors who repositioned themselves in Asian and emerging market equities earlier this month should begin reducing their risk exposure. (Jinshi)

data.title: AI Tool Breach Potentially Exposes Web3 Credentials
data.description:
A recent security breach linked to a compromised AI tool may have exposed credentials used by app frontends, which serve as the user-facing layer connecting web3 wallets and trading interfaces to backend services. According to CoinDesk, this incident raises concerns about the security of digital assets and the integrity of web3 applications. The breach highlights vulnerabilities in the integration of AI tools within the web3 ecosystem, emphasizing the need for enhanced security measures to protect sensitive information.

The compromised credentials could potentially allow unauthorized access to user accounts and trading platforms, posing significant risks to both individual users and larger financial systems. As web3 applications continue to evolve, the reliance on AI tools for seamless user experiences must be balanced with robust security protocols to prevent such breaches. This incident serves as a reminder for developers and companies to prioritize cybersecurity in the development and deployment of AI-driven solutions within the web3 space. Ensuring the protection of user data and maintaining trust in digital platforms are crucial as the industry navigates the complexities of integrating advanced technologies.

data.title: Hong Kong to Formulate AI Strategy Amid $2 Billion Tokenized Bond Issuance
data.description: Hong Kong Financial Secretary Chan Mo-po announced plans to establish an AI committee and develop an industry strategy as the issuance of tokenized green and infrastructure bonds surpasses $2 billion. According to NS3.AI, Chan emphasized the potential of AI and Web3 as transformative technologies, highlighting Hong Kong's pilot of tokenized deposit applications through the Ensemble project. He also stressed the importance of balanced AI regulation that fosters innovation while ensuring human oversight and addressing cybersecurity and fraud risks.

data.title: AI TRENDS | U.S. AI Model Call Volume Surpasses China's for First Time in Two Months
data.description: According to NS3.AI, OpenRouter data indicates that the U.S. AI large model call volume reached 4.908 trillion tokens last week, surpassing China's 4.441 trillion tokens for the first time in nearly two months. China's volume experienced a decline of 23.77% from the previous week, while the U.S. volume saw an increase of 20.62%.

data.title: The weekly usage of China's large AI models dropped by 23.8% week-on-week, with the US surpassing China for the first time in nearly two months.
data.description: According to the latest data from OpenRouter, the total global AI large-scale model calls last week (April 13-19) amounted to 20.6 trillion tokens, declining for the second consecutive week. Among the listed AI large-scale models, the weekly call volume of Chinese AI large-scale models dropped to 4.441 trillion tokens, also declining for the second consecutive week, a 23.77% decrease compared to the previous week; while the weekly call volume of US AI large-scale models was 4.908 trillion tokens, a 20.62% increase week-on-week. This marks the first time in nearly two months that the weekly call volume of US AI large-scale models has surpassed that of China. Four of the top nine global AI large-scale models in terms of call volume last week were from China. Among them, DeepSeek V3.2 ranked second with a weekly call volume of 1.28 trillion tokens; MiMo-V2-Pro ranked fourth with a weekly call volume of 1.15 trillion tokens, a 90% increase compared to the previous week; two MiniMax models made the list, with MiniMax M2.5 ranking sixth with a weekly call volume of 1.05 trillion tokens; and MiniMax M2.7 ranking seventh with a weekly call volume of 0.961 trillion tokens, a 19% decrease compared to the previous week.
